candidates.name: Ron DeSantis
candidates.notes: [2026 actions: Signed 80+ laws this session. Notable: HB 905 Foreign Interference Restriction & Enforcement Act; HB 1471 anti-Sharia + counterterrorism; SB 1134 statewide DEI ban; HB 1217 anti-net-zero / anti-carbon-tax; SB 484 data-center consumer protections. Continues to lead the country on combined Christian liberty + family + border + economic stewardship action.]

profile.religion: Catholic
profile.education: Yale University (BA, History); Harvard Law School (JD)
profile.background: Navy JAG officer who served in Iraq and at Guantanamo Bay; U.S. Representative (2013-2018); became 46th governor in 2019; ran for president in 2024.

claims.text: Signed SB 300, the Heartbeat Protection Act, prohibiting abortion after detection of fetal cardiac activity (approximately 6 weeks). Became Chapter 2023-21 of Florida law on April 14, 2023.
Florida's FY 2021–2023 budgets excluded state funding for elective abortion providers; DeSantis-era administration prioritized alternatives-to-abortion programs over reproductive-health clinics.

Signed CS/CS/HB 1557, 'Parental Rights in Education,' restricting classroom instruction on sexual orientation and gender identity in K–3 (and requiring age-appropriate limits thereafter). Became Chapter 2022-22 on March 28, 2022; effective July 1, 2022.

Signed CS/HB 543, 'Public Safety,' permitting unlicensed concealed carry of firearms for law-abiding citizens (permitless / 'constitutional carry'). Approved April 3, 2023; Chapter 2023-18; effective July 1, 2023.

Signed SB 1718 (2023), strengthening illegal-immigration enforcement: expanded E-Verify mandate for employers of 25+, criminalized transportation of illegal aliens into Florida, and invalidated out-of-state driver's licenses issued to undocumented immigrants.

Signed HB 1 (2023), making Florida the first universal-school-choice state: Empowerment Scholarship Accounts (ESAs) eligibility opened to all K-12 students regardless of public-school enrollment or family income. Approved March 27, 2023.

Signed HB 7 (2022), the 'Individual Freedom' / 'Stop WOKE' Act, restricting instruction that compels students or employees to endorse specific race/sex-based concepts. Became Chapter 2022-72 on April 22, 2022.

Signed SB 90 (2021) tightening Florida's election-integrity regime: constraints on mail-ballot drop boxes, stricter voter-ID requirements at ballot-request stage, and limits on third-party ballot collection.

claims.text: Voted yes on the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R. 26, 118th Congress, Jan 11 2023), which requires medical care for infants born alive after a failed abortion. The bill passed the House 220-210.

Cosponsored the Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act (H.R. 38, 119th Congress, 2025) — legislation requiring all states to honor concealed-carry permits issued by other states, an expansion of carry rights consistent with constitutional carry principles.

Cosponsored the Birthright Citizenship Act of 2025 (H.R. 569, 119th Congress) — a bill to end automatic birthright citizenship for children born in the U.S. to parents who are neither citizens nor lawful permanent residents.

candidates.name: Debbie Mayfield
candidates.notes: Florida State Senator, District 19 (covers most of Brevard County including Melbourne, Palm Bay, Titusville, and the Space Coast). Returned to Senate January 2025 after winning special election. Previously served FL Senate 2016-2024 and FL House 2008-2016. Heritage Action 95%+ lifetime. NRA A+. Planned Parenthood 0%. Strong on parental rights (authored Parental Rights in Education Act companion bill), pro-life, and Second Amendment. Chair of Senate Rules Committee during previous tenure.

candidates.name: Ben Albritton
candidates.notes: Elected Florida Senate President for the 2024–2026 term. Republican from central Florida (FL Senate District 27, covering Polk, Hardee, Highlands, and DeSoto counties plus parts of Okeechobee and Osceola). Former Florida House member. Strong conservative on agricultural policy, fiscal restraint, and Christian heritage themes. Signed onto priority legislation on property tax relief, hurricane recovery, and education accountability during his presidency. President of the Florida Senate — verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_the_Florida_Senate.

candidates.name: Eileen Higgins
candidates.notes: 44th Mayor of Miami (D); won Dec 9 2025 runoff 59.46% vs. Emilio Gonzalez 40.54%; sworn in Dec 18 2025; term to 2029. First woman Mayor of Miami; first non-Hispanic since 1996; first Democrat elected mayor since 1997. Former Miami-Dade County Commissioner District 5 (Jun 2018-Nov 2025; resigned to take mayoral office). Former U.S. State Dept FSO and Peace Corps Country Director (Belize). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement evidence-pass] HARD evidence (county tenure + mayoral campaign): SPONSORED 2019 Miami-Dade ordinance creating county's first codified LGBTQ Advisory Board (incl. explicit trans rep); declared countywide Transgender Day of Visibility 2024; multi-year Pride proclamation/flag-raising history at County Hall — biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE and family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE. HARD evidence on guns: longtime Moms Demand Action volunteer; Giffords-endorsed; advocated background-check expansion and against gun shows on county property — self_defense q0/q1 FALSE. HARD evidence on immigration: publicly opposes Miami's 287(g) cooperation, vowed to minimize Miami PD ICE involvement, called Alligator Alcatraz 'a sin'; quoted: 'There's no reason police in the city of Miami should be doing the job of federal law enforcement' — border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE and refuse_state_overreach q0 TRUE (asserted local control against state ICE mandate, content-neutral on direction). NO direct quote located on personhood-from-conception or abortion-abolition (Dem-coalition endorsements are indirect — held null per evidence-first discipline). NO documented position on paper ballots / voter ID / Zuckerbucks specifically — null. Religion: Catholic per secondary biographical aggregators; not confirmed via her own statement — left null in profile.

profile.background: Born June 30 1964, Dayton OH; raised Albuquerque NM. BS Mechanical Engineering (U. New Mexico), MBA (Cornell 1989). Former U.S. State Department Foreign Service Officer (Mexico, South Africa) and Peace Corps Country Director (Belize, 2006). Miami-Dade County Commissioner District 5 Jun 2018-Nov 2025 (won 2018 special election after Bruno Barreiro resigned). Elected 44th Mayor of Miami Dec 9 2025 runoff (59.46% vs. Emilio Gonzalez 40.54%); sworn in Dec 18 2025; term to 2029. First woman Mayor of Miami; first non-Hispanic since 1996; first Democrat elected mayor since 1997.

r1.title: Sponsored Oct 2019 Miami-Dade ordinance creating county's first codified LGBTQ Advisory Board (explicitly includes trans representation); regular sponsor of Pride flag-raisings at County Hall.

candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 4 (Southside), Council President 2025-26 (term to July 1, 2027). Impact Church (per his official city bio page). candidates.name: Nick Howland
candidates.office: City Council Vice President (At-Large Group 3 · sworn in as Council President June 25, 2026)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Group 3, sitting Council VP (sworn in as Council President June 25, 2026 for 2026-27 year). Filed for 2027 re-election to AL-3, unopposed as of May 2026 ($320K+ raised); Charter §5.05 two-term limit not yet triggered (2022 partial term typically doesn't count). HARD evidence (cited): As Finance Vice Chair (2025-26), voted YES on the FY2025-26 one-eighth-mill property tax rate cut (passed 10-9 Sept 23, 2025), publicly stating '$1 in the hands of an individual is better than $1 in the hands of the government' — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (affirmative fiscal-restraint posture). Sheriff's Office Council Liaison for three years; received Jacksonville Sheriff's endorsement in his 2022 special-election win; publicly states 'public safety is my top priority' — public_justice q0 TRUE. Public floor advocate for Ord. 2025-0147 (Jacksonville Illegal Immigration Enforcement Act, passed 12-5 in spring 2025), on the record: Jacksonville is 'standing with our president, with our governor, with our state legislature to secure our borders' — border_immigration q1/q2 TRUE. As Rules Committee Chair (May 2025), Howland was the first councilmember to publicly expose Jacksonville's covert City Hall gun registry (private security logging armed visitors' names and firearm types in apparent violation of FL §790.335 since July 2023); his disclosure led to a $5M state AG suit against the city and a DeSantis 'accountability' demand — self_defense q1 TRUE (opposes firearm registries; concrete affirmative-2A action). On Diamond's FY2025-26 abortion-defunding amendment, urged Diamond procedurally to withdraw and bring as standalone legislation (no substantive pro-life statement of his own) — sanctity_of_life left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on biblical_marriage, family/schools (no city-level rubric-relevant vote), christian_liberty (no cited church or invocation statement), election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, or self_defense q0 — those cells null. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND.

profile.background: B.A. Duke (1995), M.B.A. Chicago Booth, U.S. Navy officer 1995-1999. Executive in defense, security, and marine-safety industries. Executive Director, The Fire Watch (Florida veteran-suicide-prevention nonprofit). First won At-Large Group 3 in Feb 2022 special election (succeeded late Tommy Hazouri); re-elected to full term to July 1, 2027. Council Vice President 2025-26; voted in 5/26/2026 as Council President for 2026-27 year (sworn 6/25/2026). Filed for 2027 AL-3 re-election; unopposed as of May 2026 with $320K raised.

candidates.name: Rory Diamond
candidates.office: City Council Member, District 13 (Beaches), City of Jacksonville (sitting · term-limited, ends June 30, 2027 · widely reported as a likely 2027 mayoral candidate)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting District 13 council member (Beaches), term to June 30, 2027 (term-limited); widely reported as likely 2027 Jacksonville mayoral candidate. HARD evidence (cited): (a) sanctity_of_life q0 TRUE — publicly stated 'abortion to me and my family and to people who believe as I do is murder' when opposing reimbursement of city-employee abortion-travel; sponsored FY25-26 'Big Beautiful Budget Amendments' banning city funds for 'abortion and abortion-related services as defined by Florida law' — affirmative personhood-grade conviction statement. (b) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — voted YES on June 9, 2020 (15-4) to RE-CODIFY Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance protecting sexual orientation AND gender identity in employment, housing, and public accommodation (the same ordinance later used against Queen of Angels Catholic bookstore until a 2023 religious-exemption settlement). His 2025 anti-DEI budget amendments do not retroactively reverse the 2020 vote — q4 FALSE stands. (c) public_justice q0 TRUE — Jacksonville FOP endorsement in 2019; on the dais opposing 2020 defund amendments to JSO: 'To pretend that taking half the funding from our JSO is not defunding our police is just completely irrational... They're always one second away from death... I find this effort, this amendment, absolutely disgusting'; sustained K9s For Warriors veterans/LE-adjacent CEO bio (2014-2023); JAG officer bio — multiple independent affirmative-advocacy signals clearing the FXBG high bar. (d) border_immigration q2 TRUE — sponsored 2025 Jacksonville ordinance barring city grant recipients from using city money to support services for immigrants without permanent legal status; on the record: 'Jacksonville will never be a sanctuary city' and accused Mayor Deegan of 'making Jacksonville a sanctuary city under our noses' via her veto — direct anti-sanctuary, federal-preemption-aligned action. (e) self_defense q1 TRUE — publicly defended Florida's 2004 state firearms preemption statute against Jacksonville's de facto carry-permit registry (JSO carry logbooks): 'I absolutely support the law... it sure looks like it to me' (re: the AG's 2026 lawsuit) — direct opposition to a city-level gun registry under the q1 rubric scope (red-flag / mag limits / AWB / registries). NOT scored on sanctity_of_life q1 (abolition / personhood-grade legal regime): his policy posture operates within Florida's existing legal framework, not personhood/abolition. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite 1%-cap balanced-budget local legislation: q2 is narrowly about deficit spending / federal BBA, and the 1%-cap is local fiscal restraint — per the FXBG discipline that excludes 'generic low-tax claims' from q2, leave null. NOT scored on refuse_state_overreach q2 despite anti-DEI budget amendments: anti-DEI cuts are local action against local programs, not refusal of state overreach. NOT scored on self_defense q0: Florida already has constitutional carry by state law (HB 543, 2023) — Diamond defended state preemption against city registries but no direct permitless-carry statement located. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on biblical_marriage q0/q2, family/child-sovereignty, christian_liberty cells, election integrity, public_justice q1-q4 — null. Religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND (specific church not located, though his pro-life quote 'people who believe as I do' implies religiously informed conviction).

profile.background: Rory Diamond — Jacksonville City Council District 13 (Atlantic Beach, Jacksonville Beach, Neptune Beach, Mayport, Intracoastal West). Elected May 2019; re-elected 2023 (defeated write-in only). Former litigator at international firms; former Special Assistant U.S. Attorney. Captain, U.S. Army / Florida National Guard JAG — deployed to the Middle East with the 40th Infantry Division in 2023 as Chief of Military Justice; returned to council duty Feb 2024. Former CEO (2014-2023) of K9s For Warriors (national veterans / service-dog nonprofit headquartered near Jacksonville); co-authored the federal PAWS Act (Puppies Assisting Wounded Servicemembers) signed in 2021 with then-Rep. Ron DeSantis. Wife reportedly Lauren Blocker. Term-limited from D13 after 2027; widely reported considering 2027 Jacksonville mayoral run.

candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Jacksonville At-Large Group 1 council member; Council President 2022-2023; second elected term; SUBMITTED RESIGNATION May 28, 2026, formally vacates Nov 2, 2026 to run for Florida House District 12 (HD-12 R primary Aug 18, 2026, currently unopposed; special election Aug 18, 2026). candidacy_status: running_higher_office. HARD evidence (cited): (a) biblical_marriage q2 FALSE + q4 FALSE — voted YES on June 9, 2020 (15-4) on Ordinance 2020-0244 to RE-CODIFY Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance protecting LGBT INCLUDING gender identity (after FL appellate court invalidated prior version on technical grounds); did NOT join the four-member dissent (White / Becton / Ferraro / Newby). Direct affirmative vote to codify gender-identity-as-protected-class (q2 FALSE) AND to advance LGBTQ+ identity in city public policy (q4 FALSE). No subsequent repeal effort during his Council Presidency. (b) public_justice q0 TRUE — campaign-site priority: 'Supporting Our First Responders to Fight Crime and Keep Jacksonville Safe'; Council Presidency 'ensured full funding' / 'record funding' for JSO and Fire & Rescue; Sheriff T.K. Waters HD-12 endorsement: 'Terrance Freeman has always backed the men and women in uniform'; FOP Lodge 5-30 (President Randy Reaves) endorsement: 'Terrance Freeman has always stood with the men and women who put on the badge'; X post defending JSO against 'Far-Left' attacks: 'T.K. Waters is an amazing leader who Jacksonville is lucky to have as our Sheriff' — multiple affirmative-advocacy signals + LE-leadership endorsements clearing the FXBG high bar. (c) border_immigration q2 TRUE — voted YES on Ordinance 2025-0138 (passed 6/12/25), prohibiting city tax dollars going to illegal immigrants and establishing city-level immigration enforcement coordination; on the record: 'This bill was not filed to hurt people... it was filed to ensure that government fulfills its top priority, which is to keep its citizens safe'; campaign-site claim 'helped lead the charge to prohibit tax dollars from going to illegal immigrants' — affirmative anti-sanctuary / federal-preemption-aligned action. NOT scored on border_immigration q1 (mandatory deportation): ordinance is enforcement-coordination + funding-restriction, not a mandatory-deportation regime; per discipline, q1 null. NOT scored on sanctity_of_life q1: Freeman RECUSED from the FY25-26 budget vote (which included Diamond's pro-life/anti-DEI/anti-illegal-immigrant amendments) due to Miller Electric / city contract conflict — recusal is not a position; q1 null. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite Council-President millage reduction: 1/8-mill cut is local fiscal restraint, not federal BBA endorsement; per FXBG discipline q2 null. NOT scored on refuse_state_overreach: Freeman INVITED state DOGE oversight of city finances — the OPPOSITE of refusing state overreach; null. NOT scored on christian_liberty q3 despite 1/10/24 personal introduction of Hope Baptist Church Pastor Tim Nelson for council invocation: routine institutional invocation, not affirmative defense of prayer-in-public-bodies against challenge; null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life q0, biblical_marriage q0, family-child-sovereignty, christian_liberty q0/q2/q4, election integrity, public_justice q1-q4, or self_defense — null. DEVIATION ALERT: R out-of-line on biblical_marriage q2 + q4 (2020 HRO YES vote, including gender-identity protections) — significant culture-war evidence against the rubric direction. Possible name-conflation with same-named 'Terrance Freeman' principal/baseball coach at Eagle's View Academy — career details DO NOT match; do not import that bio. Religion: father Baptist minister noted in background; no personal denominational identity in cited record — religion field left null.

profile.background: Terrance Freeman — Jacksonville City Council At-Large Group 1, R; age ~51. Career: Executive Council Assistant to former Council Member Aaron Bowman starting 2015; subsequently Senior Project Manager / Senior Program Manager of Apprenticeship Programs at Miller Electric Company. Appointed by Gov. Rick Scott to Council District 10 in 2018; elected At-Large Group 1 in 2019; re-elected 2023 with 65% (100,297 votes — highest of any candidate on ballot). Council Vice President 2021; Council President 2022-2023. Chairs Rules Committee; serves on Finance Committee. Married, father of five. Bachelor's and master's degrees in physical education / sports psychology. Father was an Army veteran and Baptist minister; mother a career social worker. Submitted resignation May 28, 2026 to run for Florida House District 12; formally vacates council seat Nov 2, 2026; HD-12 Republican primary Aug 18, 2026 (currently unopposed); special election to fill council seat Aug 18, 2026.

candidates.name: Matt Carlucci
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Group 4, current term ends July 1, 2027 (TERM-LIMITED — Matt McCullough has entered race to replace him). Self-described moderate Republican; State Farm agent. HARD evidence (cited): publicly opposed Councilman Diamond's FY2025-26 budget amendment banning city funding for 'abortion and abortion-related services,' calling it a 'poison pill' of 'divisive rhetoric' and saying on the floor 'Have you no damn shame?' — sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE. Co-sponsored Ord. 2020-0244 with Aaron Bowman and LeAnna Cumber restoring LGBT anti-discrimination protections to Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance (passed 15-4 in June 2020) — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (affirmatively expanded LGBT identity in city public policy). Voted NO on the FY2025-26 one-eighth-mill property tax rate cut (10-9 passage), saying 'You can't cut your way to a greater city. I don't see waste' — local fiscal-restraint posture is opposed; left economic_stewardship q2 null (the question is narrowly about deficit spending / BBA, not millage votes). Voted NO on Ord. 2025-0138 (immigration-funding restrictions) and NO on the related FY2026 budget amendment banning city funds for 'illegal aliens,' arguing 'If they're here and they're not documented, that's for the feds to take care of … they're still human beings' — border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE. Has led council push to remove Confederate monuments (separate from rubric mapping). NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a cited church home, public statements on schools/CRT/SOGI/parental rights, invocation policy, election integrity, public-justice affirmative-advocacy, refuse-state-overreach, or 2A — those cells null.

profile.background: State Farm insurance agent (Hendricks Ave) since 1981. Fifth term overall (1987-1994, 1999-2003, 2019-present); current At-Large Group 4 term to July 1, 2027 (term-limited). Council President 2001. Appointed by Gov. Rick Scott to FL Commission on Ethics; chaired Commission 2016-2017. Self-described moderate Republican.

candidates.name: Buddy Dyer
candidates.office: Mayor of Orlando, Florida (sitting since March 2003 · longest-serving mayor in city history · re-elected Nov 2023 with 72.8% for 7th term · stated final term · term to Jan 10, 2028 · 2027 race is open seat)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Mayor of Orlando as of June 2026 (orlando.gov bio + Nov 2023 7th-term result + ongoing 2025-26 mayoral statements). 23 years on the job — extensive documented record across the rubric. Sweep of orlando.gov, Orlando Sentinel, Orlando Weekly, ClickOrlando WKMG, WFTV, WESH, Spectrum News 13, CFPublic, WUSF, Florida Politics, Advocate, CNN, NBC News, Tampa Bay Times, Mayors Against Illegal Guns roster, Frost.house.gov, Demings.house.gov, Ballotpedia. HARD evidence — 2 TRUE / 9 FALSE. TRUE: economic_stewardship q2 (12 consecutive years flat millage at 6.65 mills incl. FY26 budget per CFPublic 7/15/2025 — documented restraint posture, though rollback-vs-nominal analysis not surfaced this batch); public_justice q0 (affirmative OPD funding advocacy — publicly thanked Rep. Demings for $1.2M OPD body-cam grant; FY26 budget added 20 OPD employees; routinely appears alongside OPD Chief Eric Smith at briefings — cross-direction for a long-serving urban D). FALSE: biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 (served as Grand Marshal of 2016 Come Out With Pride parade; defended FDOT's removal of rainbow Pulse crosswalk as 'cruel political act' in Aug 2025; repeated 'welcoming for LGBTQ+ residents' framing through 2025 Pulse-9 anniversary statement); christian_liberty q4 (under Dyer Orlando flies Pride flag + hosts Pride proclamations / events); public_justice q3 (joined Rep. Maxwell Frost at Office of Gun Violence Prevention presser framing violence as public-health crisis); refuse_state_overreach q2 (declared early COVID state of emergency March 2020; co-led Orange Co mask mandate that persisted past DeSantis Phase 3 reopening); border_immigration q1 + q2 (2018 Orlando 'Trust Act' bars OPD from immigration inquiries — sanctuary-style policy; April 2026 City Council 'unequivocally' opposed local ICE facility); self_defense q0 + q1 (Mayors Against Illegal Guns member + signed 2020 FL mayors letter urging universal background checks + publicly called for ban on 'assault weapons, large capacity magazines and bump stocks' alongside Frost). NULL HOLDS (downgraded from agent's call per skill discipline): sanctity_of_life q1 NULL (agent suggested FALSE on party-pattern inference but no direct quote citing abortion-abolition opposition — kept honest); public_justice q1 NULL (silence on SA-9 Worrell ≠ documented contrary position); refuse_state_overreach q0 NULL (rubric specifies 'UNCONSTITUTIONAL state mandates' — Dyer's resistance is to constitutional state law on sanctuary + Pride crosswalk, opposite cultural direction from rubric intent). After AG Uthmeier removal threat April 2025, Dyer signaled compliance with state immigration law + entered 287(g) training — i.e., backed down rather than refused enforcement (refuse_state_overreach q4 NULL).

profile.religion: No public faith profession located
profile.background: John Hugh 'Buddy' Dyer (b. Aug 7, 1958, Kissimmee FL). BA civil engineering Brown University; JD University of Florida College of Law. Civil engineer / attorney by training. FL State Senate 1992-2002 (Senate Minority Leader 1998-2000); 2002 Democratic nominee for FL Attorney General (lost to Charlie Crist). Mayor of Orlando since March 2003 — 48th and longest-serving mayor. Divorced Karen Caudill 2017 (2 sons); remarried Susie Pinson Galloway Shields 2021. 2005 absentee-ballot fraud charge dismissed; reinstated by Gov. Bush. Central public figure in 2016 Pulse nightclub aftermath.

candidates.name: Tom Keen
candidates.notes: Orlando City Commissioner, District 1 (NP ballot; personal Democrat registration, party-switcher from R); sworn Jan 12, 2026, term to Jan 2030. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence pulled from his 2024 FL House campaign + 2025 D1 endorsement slate (only ~5 months in office, so no Orlando council roll-call yet on values cells): (a) endorsed by Florida Planned Parenthood; campaigned on 'restoring' abortion access and supported Amendment 4 (would have enshrined abortion to viability ~24 weeks in FL Constitution); criticized FL six-week ban — sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE; (b) endorsed by Equality Florida Action PAC in both 2024 House race and 2025 Orlando D1 race; self-described 'staunch advocate for LGBTQIA+ rights' — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE; (c) campaigned on 'keeping books on shelves' opposing library book removals; aligned with Eskamani / Carlos G. Smith on SOGI — family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE; (d) endorsed by Giffords (gun-control PAC) in 2024 House race; supports universal background checks and strengthening FL's red-flag law — self_defense q1 FALSE. NULL on Christian liberty, economic stewardship (FY25-26 Orlando budget adopted Sept 2025 before he was seated; no FY27 vote yet), election integrity, public_justice (review-board service is oversight not affirmative backing), refuse_state_overreach, and border (Keen not individually quoted in Jan/Apr 2026 Orlando ICE-facility coverage). Religion not publicly stated.

profile.background: Charles Thomas 'Tom' Keen III. Orlando City Commissioner, District 1 (officially nonpartisan ballot); personal voter registration Democrat (previously Republican, party-switcher). Sworn in January 12, 2026 after defeating incumbent Jim Gray 53.97%-31.25% in November 4, 2025 election. Prior offices: Florida House District 35 (Jan 27, 2024 – Nov 5, 2024; lost rematch to Erika Booth 48.02%-51.98%); 2017 Orlando D1 race (lost to Gray); 2022 FL House D35 Democratic primary (lost by 57 votes); 2012 IN State Senate D16 (lost). Bachelor's degree, University of Cincinnati. U.S. Navy retired Commander (O-5); Naval Flight Officer designated 1983; airborne reconnaissance with shore tour at U.S. Embassy London. Post-military: manager at Collins Aerospace. Previously served on Orlando Citizens' Police Review Board and Mayor's Veterans Advisory Council. Current 4-year term: Jan 12, 2026 – Jan 2030.

candidates.name: Tony Ortiz
candidates.notes: Orlando City Commissioner D2 since 2008 (NP ballot, personal voter reg R→D May 6, 2025); term to Jan 10, 2028; considering 2027 mayoral bid. profile.background: Esteban 'Steve' Bovo Jr., b. June 12, 1962 in Queens, NY to Esteban Bovo Carás (2506 Brigade / Bay of Pigs veteran). AA Business Administration, Miami-Dade Community College (1983); BS Political Science, Florida International University (1987). Pre-politics: marketing/advertising executive, Hialeah Park Race Track. Career: Hialeah City Council 1998-2008; Florida House District 110 (2008-2011, resigned to run for county); Miami-Dade County Commissioner District 13 (2011-2020, served as Chair); ran for Miami-Dade Mayor 2020 (won R primary, lost general); elected Mayor of Hialeah Nov 2, 2021; RESIGNED April 27, 2025 to join The Southern Group lobbying firm in D.C., following wife Viviana Bovo (longtime Marco Rubio staffer, now State Department). Five children: Oscar, Bianca, Esteban, Sofia, Alessandro. Interim Mayor Jacqueline Garcia-Roves (City Council President) sworn in April 2025 — Hialeah's first female mayor; special election Nov 2025.

profile.background: Gregory Scott Tony (D, b. 1978 Philadelphia) — 17th Sheriff of Broward County, first Black sheriff in county history. Appointed by Gov. candidates.name: Wayne Ivey
candidates.notes: HIGH-DEVIATION-RICH archetype-5 'Constitutional Sheriff' profile — Trump-aligned + 2A maximalist + anti-sanctuary + anti-mandate + openly Christian framing. 14 TRUE cells across PJ/RSO/BI/SD/CL/FCS clusters. HEADLINE TRUE CLUSTER: (1) RSO 5/5 TRUE — 2013 signed statement refusing federal gun-control enforcement, 'we are not the mask police' Oct 2021 deputies-will-not-enforce-school-masks, 'stands with America's workforce in fight against vaccine mandates' Oct 2021, rebuked DOJ for 'threats of violence' framing of school-board-protest parents, 'Constitutional Sheriff' public identity; (2) BI 2/2 TRUE — 'You bring that crap to Brevard County, and you're going to end up in jail' anti-sanctuary, Brevard launched 287(g) in 2019 (pre-DeSantis statewide), Feb 2025 joined DeSantis Illegal Immigration Street Task Force, BCSO awarded ~$700K immigration-enforcement funding Feb 2026; (3) SD 2/2 TRUE — viral 'arm up and prepare for an attack' video calling on citizens to arm themselves, attended April 2023 constitutional carry signing 'Criminals don't go get a permit,' Sept 2025 open-carry message announcing deputies will not enforce ban after 1st DCA ruling; (4) PJ 4/5 TRUE — Wheel of Fugitive + chain-gang program (modeled on Arpaio 2013) + 'encouraging citizens to arm themselves and confront bad guys before deputies arrive' (Marshall Project), 'zero tolerance for crime keeps FL safe' flvoicenews, June 2025 anti-riot 'we will kill you, graveyard dead' press conference with AG Uthmeier, right-wing-law-enforcement-ideology alignment; (5) CL 3/4 TRUE — Defended 'In God We Trust' patrol-car decals on Fox & Friends Nov 2019, rebuked DOJ school-board-parents-as-threats memo, spoke at Trinity Wellsprings Church Men's Ministry Breakfast March 2020 'about his faith and protecting the foundation of the nation'; (6) FCS q0 TRUE — 'We are not the mask police' framed around parents' authority over their children. SINGLE FALSE DEVIATION: PJ q4 — Gregory Edwards 2018 in-custody death (Army combat vet, restraint chair, pepper spray, spit hood, stun-gun barbs in back); BCSO fought 2+ years to release jail video, FLORIDA TODAY had to sue under Public Records Act (released Nov 2020); federal §1983 force claims dismissed Mar 2023 but state negligence claim survived — pattern of accountability resistance, not reform. SOL all NULL — sheriff lane discipline; no abortion statement located; do NOT infer from R-party or evangelical framing. BM all NULL — no specific marriage/SOGI/Pride statement located. EC q2 NULL — no documented fiscal-restraint posture in agency. CAREFUL DATA HYGIENE: research agent honestly flagged that prior batch-prompt confused (a) Marion County's Susan Lorincz/Santiago-Miranda case (Sheriff Billy Woods, NOT Brevard), (b) GA Laken Riley case (Jose Ibarra) with the Brevard Aug 2021 Paris Wilder traffic-stop ambush (deputy Brian Potters wounded NOT killed) — neither belongs to Ivey's record; not scored.

candidates.name: Paul Alfrey
candidates.office: Mayor of Melbourne, Florida (sitting · 2nd term, ends Nov 2028; current charter is 2 consecutive 4-year terms)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Mayor of Melbourne, FL (2nd term to Nov 2028); not on 2026 ballot. Party R (Republican Mayors Association affiliate publicly recognized 2024 re-election). HARD evidence (cited): In Sept 2022 publicly defended the Space Coast Pride permit — which included a drag queen story time component — against State Rep. Randy Fine's demand the city pull the permit, posting on Facebook that drag queen story time 'is an event that happens nationwide and is attended by both adults and children' and that the organizers 'had as much right to organize as any.' This is affirmative defense of city-permitted LGBT promotion in public policy against a same-party state-rep challenge — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE. In Feb 2022 personally led Melbourne's 'Just Say No' anti-panhandling public-safety campaign rolled out as a Mayor+MPD partnership ('Just say no' message + city plan); 15-year retired Melbourne PD officer (K-9, Street Crimes, Community Policing, FTO) whose LE identity is centerpiece of his official bio; in June 2020 publicly defended the office of LE while distancing from a single FOP-president-personal-speech incident ('whoever put this up... they need to go') — affirmative pro-LE public-safety advocacy under the same standard applied to Vice Mayor Neuman's Jan 2025 MPD-partnership homelessness resolution → public_justice q0 TRUE. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite his '10-year low millage' boast: the question is narrowly about deficit spending / BBA, not generic low-tax claims. In Sept 2025 personally proposed renaming a stretch of Cypress Avenue to 'Charlie Kirk Lane' after Kirk's assassination ('He will not be forgotten in Melbourne!') and promoted 12+ Brevard County memorial billboards; council voted 5-1 against (only Vice Mayor Neuman supported). This is ideological signal but does not cleanly map to a christian_liberty rubric cell (Kirk Lane is a memorial naming of a Christian-affiliated public figure, not a Christian symbol or prayer policy) → christian_liberty cells null per the no-overread rule. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life, transgender ideology, family/parental rights/schools (city does not run schools), invocation/Nativity policy, election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, sanctuary/ICE cooperation, or 2A — those cells null. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND. DEVIATION ALERT (R out-of-line on biblical_marriage q4).

profile.background: Retired law enforcement (3 years Sanford PD, 12 years Melbourne PD — K-9, Street Crimes, Community Policing, FTO; retired 2012). Florida Certified Contractor, Home Inspector, Insurance Agent; small-business owner. Eau Gallie HS; Seminole State College of Florida; Miami Dade College. First elected to City Council District 5 in 2016 (served as Vice Mayor); elected Mayor November 2020; re-elected November 5, 2024 with 50.84% (19,142 votes) over former mayor Kathy Meehan (25.92%) and Hazel Buggs (23.24%). Sits on Melbourne Orlando International Airport Authority Board and Melbourne Affordable Housing Board.

candidates.name: Thad Altman
candidates.notes: MIXED MODERATE-R PROFILE — 30+ year FL legislator with substantial pro-life + parental-rights + E-Verify signature WITH meaningful red-flag-gun-control + universal-voucher-skeptic deviations. HARD-TRUE CLUSTER (5): (1) sanctity_of_life q0+q1 — HB 5 2022 15-week abortion ban YES + HB 1429 2018 dismemberment-abortion ban YES (consistent pro-life voting); (2) biblical_marriage q2+q4 + family q0+q2 — HB 1557 2022 Parental Rights in Education ('Don't Say Gay') YES (signature parental-rights + biological-sex + SOGI-curriculum-opposition vote); (3) border q2 — HB 89 2018 E-Verify LEAD SPONSOR + anti-sanctuary HB 9 cosponsor (strong border/sanctuary-opposition signature). HARD-FALSE CLUSTER (2 significant deviations): (1) family q1 (school choice) — voted NO on HB 1 2023 universal-voucher expansion per Freedom Index, breaking with DeSantis-era FL GOP consensus on universal school choice (notable break for a long-tenured R); (2) self_defense q0+q1 — voted YES on SB 7026 2018 Parkland Marjory Stoneman Douglas Public Safety Act (raised long-gun age to 21, created red-flag/risk-protection orders, 3-day waiting period) + publicly DEFENDED his support including the red-flag provisions in 2023 Phoenix retrospective. ECON q2 FALSE — career Kennedy Space Center pork-advocacy is his signature legislative legacy (his Wikipedia identifies space-industry funding as defining issue), not budget-restraint. PUBLIC JUSTICE all NULL — no direct 'publicly backed police' affirmative statement located meeting the LOCAL-skill HIGH-BAR requirement (voting a budget that funds police is NOT enough); HB 1 2021 anti-riot individual vote not located. CHRISTIAN LIBERTY all NULL — no specific religious-liberty votes located. ELECTION INTEGRITY q1 NULL — SB 90 2021 and SB 524 2022 specific votes not located individually despite being in House. REFUSE_STATE q0-q4 ALL NULL — only ~5 months in county office; no Brevard BoCC RSO-relevant vote located (Bill of Rights Sanctuary County December 2024 ordinance was pulled by Feltner before vote per Yahoo News; Brevard 2025 millage vote not pulled). State-leg HB 1B Nov 2021 anti-vaccine-mandate Special Session YES vote is REFUSE-FEDERAL not REFUSE-STATE territory — kept honest-NULL on RSO. Catholic religion per Vote Smart bio; Endorsed Terry Cronin March 2025 as 'Trump Republican.' Estimated net /100: ~28-35 — moderate-R archetype with pro-life + HB 1557 + E-Verify TRUE cluster offset by red-flag + universal-voucher-NO + space-pork FALSE deviations.

profile.background: Tim Bobanic (R) — Brevard County Supervisor of Elections since October 1 2022 (appointed by Governor Ron DeSantis after Lori Scott's retirement). Won 2024 R primary 59.62%-40.38% over outgoing County Commissioner John Tobia; advanced unopposed after write-in Sandra Cottrell withdrew. Religion not findable in public bio.

candidates.name: Rachel Sadoff
candidates.office: Clerk of the Circuit Court and Comptroller, Brevard County
candidates.notes: ARCHETYPE: career-administrator R-comptroller continuing Scott-Ellis-watchdog tradition; SINGLE HARD-TRUE on economic_stewardship q2 for the 2022 Lober purchasing-card audit. Headline finding: After unanimous BoCC request February 22 2022, Sadoff conducted full audit of Commissioner Bryan Lober (R, then-District 2)'s ~$40K p-card spending. Final audit concluded card use 'was often indiscriminate, wasteful, and not for a legitimate public use.' Over $9,000 unaccounted-for items; suspected misuse of tax-exemption certificate; personal email used to evade public-records law. Referred to FDLE for criminal investigation November 2022. When Lober personally requested she NOT audit him, Sadoff publicly disclosed the request: 'I would never let a friendship influence how myself or my office exercises authority under the law' (Space Coast Rocket, 2022). She also rejected suggestion that contracted auditors are preferable to an elected comptroller answerable to voters. Sadoff v. Board of County Commissioners of Brevard County (2022) appellate-court litigation exists per FindLaw — confirming her willingness to take BoCC to court as Comptroller. Stated mission to 'expand upon Clerk Ellis's legacy of transparency, accountability, and accessibility' — Ellis was the famously combative gadfly; Sadoff has delivered. CONSTITUTIONAL-OFFICE ARCHETYPE BREAK: unlike the other 3 Brevard constitutional officers in this batch (Blickley/Cullen/Ivey-prior who are roster_verified-only), Sadoff has produced a named-target, named-amount, public-record fiscal-watchdog action against a fellow Republican commissioner — a real on-record-deviation TRUE on economic_stewardship q2. Biblical marriage q0 honest-NULL: was deputy clerk (Viera branch manager) in 2015 when Obergefell came down, NOT a policymaker; Brevard's then-Clerk Scott Ellis quietly complied (no public refusal incident comparable to Baker Co Stacie Harvey or KY Kim Davis); no Sadoff public statement on Obergefell, same-sex marriage policy, or conscience-refusal located. Catholic-affiliation is proxy-strength (St Joseph Catholic School board + Barry MPA at Adrian-Dominican Catholic institution), not self-declared. Other categories NULL per office-scope + no findable positions.

profile.religion: Catholic (proxy: Saint Joseph Catholic School board service + Barry University MPA — not self-declared)
profile.background: Brevard County Clerk of the Circuit Court and Comptroller since January 1 2021 (sworn ad interim Jan 1 2021 after predecessor Scott Ellis resigned Dec 31 2020; officially sworn Jan 5 2021). First female Brevard Clerk in 167 years. Dual-hat role: Clerk + County Comptroller + Auditor + County Recorder. Career deputy clerk: started Brevard Clerk's office 1997 under Sandy Crawford; rose to Viera Branch Manager (Viera + Brevard County Jail). First elected 2020 unopposed after Sandra Ruizzo failed to qualify in GOP primary. Re-elected unopposed 2024 through December 31 2028. EFSC (B.A. Professional Studies); Barry University (MPA). Married to Steve Sadoff; three children. Board service: Saint Joseph Catholic School (Palm Bay), Space Coast Marine Institute youth diversion program, Bays Florida. Official bio language: family 'values public service, veterans, and law enforcement.'

profile.background: Lisa Cullen, CFC, is the elected constitutional officer Tax Collector of Brevard County, Florida (Republican). Born in Logan, West Virginia; family relocated to Brevard County early 1980s. Education: Merritt Island High School; A.A. Brevard Community College / Eastern Florida State College; B.S. Accounting & Finance, University of Central Florida. Career: began at the Brevard Tax Collector's Office front counter in 1985; Certified Florida Collector (CFC) since February 2009. First elected November 2008; assumed office January 2009; re-elected unopposed in 2012, 2016, and 2020. August 20, 2024 Republican primary: defeated County Commissioner Rita Pritchett 84.8% to 15.9%; write-in opponent Daniel Edwin Lopez withdrew early September 2024 making her re-elected unopposed in the general for a term to January 2029. President of the Florida Tax Collectors Association 2014-2015. Married to high-school sweetheart Robert Cullen, a RETIRED Brevard County Sheriff's Office Lieutenant. EFSC Foundation Board (2012-2018, Board Chair); Michael-Gene Basketball Foundation; National Homeless Veterans Support. 2016 TDC Portrait of Excellence; 2022 EFSC Distinguished Alumni. Rated 'A' by Ultra MAGA Patriots Brevard County (May 2024).

profile.background: Dana Blickley is the elected constitutional officer Property Appraiser of Brevard County, Florida (Republican). First elected November 2012 defeating incumbent Jim Ford by 14 points in the GOP primary; re-elected unopposed in 2016, 2020, and 2024; current term runs through January 2029. Education: BA and MA in Public Administration, University of Central Florida. Certified Florida Appraiser (CFA). 30+ years in mass appraisal / assessment administration; worked 20 years inside BCPAO (fired in 2006 by then-incumbent Ford). Brevard County resident 30+ years; married with two children; father was a Marine. Civic: board member of National Veterans Homeless Support (NVHS) since 2012; established a scholarship endowment for veterans at Eastern Florida State College. 2012 candidacy backed by the Liberty Catalyst Fund (Republican Liberty Caucus of Central East Florida + Space Coast Patriots + Brevard 9/12 grassroots coalition). Active 2025 speaking circuit: Space Coast Patriots, Merritt Island civic groups, Viera Q&A, Republican Women's Federation.

profile.religion: No public faith profession located
profile.background: Mike Jaffe — 20+ year Palm Bay resident. Director of Operations for a regional home builder; small-business owner; licensed FL realtor. Married 22 years to high-school sweetheart Kim; two children (ages 9 and 7). Little League coach. Self-described fiscal conservative focused on infrastructure (flooding / Indian River Lagoon), public safety budget protection, and responsible development. Reports knocking 5,000+ doors during 2024 campaign. Won Seat 5 with 44.24% on Nov 5, 2024 (5-way special election); sworn in + unanimously elevated to Deputy Mayor Nov 20, 2024 (Hammer nominated). Authored the Oct 17, 2025 motion to censure colleague Chandler Langevin (3-2) over Langevin's 'Deport every Indian immediately' remarks and citation of Robert Rundo, requesting Gov. DeSantis suspend Langevin under FS 112.51 + an ethics complaint. Soft-R signal: spoke at the Brevard Republican Party Straw Poll (June 22, 2024); no primary-source party registration confirmed. Announced re-election bid July 1, 2025.

candidates.name: Kenny Johnson
candidates.office: City Council Seat 4 / Deputy Mayor designate, City of Palm Bay, Florida (first elected 2018; re-elected Nov 2022; current term to Nov 2026; Seat 4 on Nov 3, 2026 regular ballot — re-election bid filed via johnson4palmbay.com)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Seat 4 Councilman of Palm Bay as of June 2026 (palmbayfl.gov staff directory + johnson4palmbay.com re-election campaign site + Hometown News Brevard 2021 profile + multiple 2024-2026 news mentions). PARTY NOTE: Palm Bay city offices are officially nonpartisan; Johnson himself has not publicly stated a party label, but his civic activity is openly Democratic-aligned (FL Democratic Party / BrevardDems hosted 'Palm Bay Knockathon with Kenny Johnson' voter-canvass events at precincts 310, 313, 510) — left party field NULL absent primary-source registration confirmation. HARD evidence — 3 TRUE / 0 FALSE / 27 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: economic_stewardship.2 (at Sept 8 2025 budget hearing Johnson + Hammer advocated for a LOWER millage; Johnson on record city must 'trim fat' when 'things get tight for the residents' and pushed 'zero-based budgeting'; voted Aye on the compromise 6.7000 rate — affirmative restraint posture beyond a routine vote, cross-direction for a D-aligned councilor); public_justice.0 (campaign site / city bio documents Johnson 'led efforts in funding our police and fire departments, along with creating new community policing programs that have kept crime rates low and increased trust in law enforcement'; statements at the Compound-crime workshop pushed police-led safety solutions; Aye on $78,500 police staffing study Feb 5 2026; Compound rezoning push tied directly to public-safety justification — affirmative advocacy beyond a budget vote); border_immigration.2 (Aye on unanimous 5-0 Palm Bay 287(g) MOA April 3 2025 — affirmative anti-sanctuary cooperation, cross-direction for a D-aligned councilor). All other 26 cells NULL after exhaustive sweep — no Pride/drag/SOGI vote at Palm Bay during his tenure, no abortion/personhood statement, no election-integrity statement, no 2A statement (FL preempts most local gun regulation), no broader refuse-state-overreach or border-immigration cells. DEVIATION ALERT (D-aligned with R-coded votes): Johnson is openly D-aligned but VOTES consistently fiscally-conservative (pushed below-staff millage, 'trim fat,' zero-based budgeting) and voted YES on 287(g) ICE MOA — exactly the cross-pressure profile the brand should surface honestly.

profile.religion: Christian — publicly self-identified: 'committed to aligning with individuals who dedicate their talents and skills to advance God's purpose and fulfill the Great Commission'; mission 'to bring unity within the body of Christ, fostering connections that are grounded in love, service, and faith.' Fellowship of Christian Athletes 2005-2012; has spoken at Greater Palm Bay Church of God events. Denomination not specified.
profile.background: Kenny Johnson, MPA — Melbourne, FL native; Bayside High School; full football scholarship to Charleston Southern University (defensive back); B.S. Criminal Justice; Master of Public Administration. Member of Omega Psi Phi Fraternity. Returned to Brevard 2013; juvenile case manager, juvenile detention officer, teacher, coach, personal trainer. Founded Top Notch Training of Brevard, Inc. (2016) and the 'Top Notch Leaders' mentoring program for 5th-6th-grade boys. First elected to Palm Bay Council Seat 4 in 2018 (~58%); re-elected 2022. Currently Deputy Mayor (rotating peer designation, sequence overlaps with Jaffe) + Chair of the Florida League of Cities Finance & Taxation Policy Committee. Established Palm Bay's Annual Black History Event, Inaugural Veterans Day Parade, Youth Advisory Board, and Civil Citation Program for misdemeanor cannabis (diversion alternative to arrest). Father; local business owner. Notably feuded with then-State Rep Randy Fine (2022 confrontation; State Attorney declined to charge Johnson — 'no prosecutable crimes').

profile.religion: Christian — explicitly professed: 'I will choose God over humanism, our children and parents over indoctrination, and our brave law enforcement officers' (STARRS interview). Denomination not specified.
profile.background: Chandler Langevin — Brevard County native, Titusville High grad. ~10 years U.S. Navy Hospital Corpsman (FMF) attached to USMC — NATO Liaison with 4th ANGLICO in Afghanistan; sniper-platoon medic with 1st Bn 25th Marines in the Pacific. Took early honorable discharge over the Biden COVID vaccine mandate ('unwillingness to be a guinea pig'). Works in veteran advocacy; founded local youth program 'Florida Scouts'; State Director, American Restoration Coalition. Previously on Odyssey Charter School board (removed Oct 2025 over the censure controversy). Briefly filed for FL HD-33 in 2023 before withdrawing and endorsing Monique Miller. Active with Republican Liberty Caucus (Brevard chapter), Moms for Liberty (Brevard), and Space Coast Young Republicans. CENSURE / LITIGATION FACTS (recorded candidly per brand voice): On Oct 17, 2025 Palm Bay Council voted 3-2 to censure Langevin and ask Gov. DeSantis to suspend him under FS 112.51 after Langevin posted on X 'Deport every Indian immediately,' Confederate-soldier-praise posts including a description of Robert E. Lee as 'a Godly and honorable presence,' and a request on city letterhead asking President Trump to pardon white-nationalist activist Robert Rundo (founder of the Rise Above Movement). Langevin filed a federal First Amendment suit (Langevin v. Palm Bay) represented by Anthony Sabatini; bench trial began Jan 21, 2026.

profile.background: Michael C. 'Mike' Blake is the elected at-large Mayor of the City of Cocoa, Florida (Brevard County, pop ~17K), and a lifelong Cocoa resident. He won re-election Nov 5, 2024 defeating George Allen Cowart 62.66% (5,081) to 37.34% (3,028) for a term running through the next regular city election in 2028. His tenure on Cocoa's elected body spans 17+ years: City Council 1998-2001, Mayor 2004-2012 (two terms), District 1 Councilman 2014-June 2016, and Mayor again from 2020 to present. By profession he is a retired Cocoa High School educator (retired early 2022). Outside city hall he has chaired the Cocoa Community Redevelopment Agency and Cocoa Housing Authority, led the Space Coast League of Cities, sat on the Florida Municipal Insurance Trust Board, and rose through the Florida League of Cities (FLC) ladder: 2nd Vice President 2022-23, 1st Vice President 2023-24, and FLC President 2024-25. FLC named him a Home Rule Hero in 2023, 2025, and 2026 for advocacy during legislative sessions to push back state preemption of municipal authority. Religion / family / military / education credentials are NOT FOUND in any public source reviewed.

profile.background: Wesley 'Wes' Morrison (age ~41) is a third-generation Space Coast Floridian whose grandfather managed one of Cape Canaveral's first grocery stores in 1965. Titusville High School graduate; B.A. Political Science (Business minor), Florida State University 2004-2008 (first college graduate in his family). Professionally a Solar Project Developer at Liberty Watt; SEIA member, Top 500 Solar Contractors 2015-2016. Two terms on Cape Canaveral City Council before being elected the city's 2nd-youngest Mayor in November 2021. Re-elected November 5, 2024 with 77.09% (2,477 votes) over Angela Raymond (22.91% / 736 votes); current 3-year term runs 2024-2027. Chairs the city's Community Redevelopment Agency Board. profile.background: Maria 'Mickie' Kellum is a FORMER Mayor Pro Tem / City Council member, City of Cape Canaveral, Florida. profile.background: Kim Davis is City Council member, City of Cape Canaveral, Florida (Democrat). NOT to be confused with Kim Davis the Rowan County KY clerk who refused same-sex marriage licenses in 2015. Florida native; one of the first babies born at Cape Canaveral Hospital; retired Brevard County Sheriff's Office deputy (served Cape Canaveral in early 1980s); former small-business owner; mother of a child with a disability. Party affiliation Democrat per Brevard County Democratic Executive Committee Elected Officials page. First elected and reelected in 2022 against Mickie Kellum + Gregory McDonald + Carroll Sanders for two seats; won. 2022 term implies term-end ~November 2025; she remains listed on city site in 2026. Civic record: Cape Canaveral Advisory Board for Culture and Leisure Services; Project Manager/Treasurer Ais Village Trail Public Archaeology Organization; former President VFW Auxiliary; Junior Vice Auxiliary Post 348 American Legion; Trustee VFW Auxiliary; Board member Space Coast Little League. Currency caveat: 2024 Cape Canaveral community FB-group post 'Councilmember Kim Davis suffers stroke' noted but city page still lists her in June 2026; no public evidence of resignation found — flagged for next sweep verification.

profile.background: Kay Jackson is a Cape Canaveral resident of 19+ years; former Network Engineering Director with Silicon Valley/global tech and project-management background; currently a small-business owner providing property-management services and tech contract work. Serves on the city's Community Redevelopment Agency Board ex officio as a council member. Stated 2023 campaign platform was 'Accountability, Fiscal Responsibility, Community, Quality of Life — For the Citizens!', emphasizing fiscally-responsible budgeting, protecting the Indian River Lagoon, balancing tourism vs. residential quality of life, and prioritizing infrastructure and citizen concerns. Won November 2023 Cape Canaveral City Council seat with 774 votes / 34.93% in a 3-way race vs. incumbent Don Willis and Steven Stroud; 3-year term so her seat is up Nov 2026. Part of the Morrison/Kellum/Jackson 3-vote bloc that in January 2025 voted 3-2 'with cause' to terminate 23-year City Manager Todd Morley, denying him ~$61K severance. Religion: NOT FOUND. Party (registration): NOT FOUND — not on the BREC endorsed-candidate roster, nor on the Brevard Dems elected-officials page; nonpartisan office and personal voter-reg party not surfaced in public search.

candidates.name: Don Willis
candidates.office: City Council, City of Cape Canaveral
candidates.notes: ARCHETYPE: Republican-aligned, business-executive, 'Home Rule + Law-and-Order' local-government technocrat — affirmatively pro-police (cleared HIGH BAR via personal Coffee-with-a-Cop sponsorship + joint BCSO LE-fraud presentation + 'Law and Order' campaign platform) and demonstrably anti-state-preemption (2025 FLC Home Rule Hero), with a near-total NULL footprint on the cultural cells. Three HARD TRUE: (1) public_justice.q0 — he and wife Paula are NAMED sponsors of BCSO 'Coffee with a Cop' (per his own city bio: 'sponsoring Coffee with a Cop'), affirmatively hosted joint Council Member + BCSO LE-fraud public-safety presentation on Identity Theft/Fraud/Scams, and campaign explicitly platformed on 'Law and Order, keeping citizens safe from crime on our streets' — pattern of affirmative engagement beyond routine budget votes meets HIGH BAR; (2) refuse_state_overreach.q0 — 2025 Florida League of Cities 'Home Rule Hero' Award recipient explicitly recognized for advocacy DURING the 2025 Florida Legislative Session against state preemption of local authority; (3) refuse_state_overreach.q3 — same Home Rule Hero designation + active service on FLC Legislative Policy Committee and Federal Action Strike Team is by definition subsidiarity advocacy (lowest-competent-level decision-making vs. state/federal preemption). 27 NULL — quiet civility-coded transportation-and-budget-focused councilmember whose only RESOLUTE-visible profile is PJ.q0 + refuse_q0 + refuse_q3. No FALSE-cluster — zero documented deviations against the rubric on culture-war cells, but silence is not affirmation in a small ~10K coastal city where most council business is stormwater, code enforcement, and CRA millage rather than life/marriage/gender. Faction signal: voted to RETAIN City Manager Morley in the Jan 2025 3-2 firing vote (institutional/establishment side of the controversy rather than reform/firing-faction).

profile.background: Don Willis is a retired business executive and entrepreneur with 40+ years professional experience; owned Porter Willis (technology consulting) for ~14 years serving international corporations and small businesses. BA Magna Cum Laude, University of North Carolina; Executive Education, UNC Kenan-Flagler Business School; PMP-certified. Before council, served as the city's Parks and Recreation Commissioner, Vice-Chair of the Business and Economic Development Advisory Board (4 years), and on a private school's board of directors. Married to Paula Willis; three grown children, three grandchildren. Elected to Cape Canaveral City Council November 2021 (top vote share ~25.4% of 5,174 votes), re-elected November 2023 with 38.81% (~860 votes). Currently serves as Treasurer of the Space Coast League of Cities; member of the Florida League of Cities Transportation/Intergovernmental Relations/Mobility/Emergency Management Legislative Committee, Cape Canaveral Local Roads Committee, Space Coast TPO Governing Board, FDOT District 5 Central Florida Safety Strategic Plan Steering Committee, and FLC Federal Action Strike Team. Recipient of the 2025 Florida League of Cities 'Home Rule Hero' Award for advocacy during the 2025 Legislative Session. Personally aligned with the Brevard Republican Executive Committee (party 'R' set on basis of 2021 BREC YouTube address). Religion: NOT FOUND (served on board of an unnamed private school; denomination not specified; the LinkedIn 'Don Willis - Pastor at CrossBridge Baptist' result is a DIFFERENT person, NOT this Don Willis). Part of the Davis-Willis 2-vote minority that voted to RETAIN City Manager Todd Morley in the 3-2 January 2025 termination vote.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — Mayor of Miami. Archetype: establishment_r.
profile.background: Retired U.S. Army colonel (26 years). Director, U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services under President George W. Bush (Dec 2005-Apr 2008). CEO, Miami International Airport (2013-2017). Miami City Manager (Jan 2018-Feb 2020, under Mayor Francis Suarez). PhD International Relations, U. Miami. Cuban-born, raised Tampa. Ran for Miami Mayor in 2025 with endorsements from Trump, DeSantis, Cruz, Rick Scott; successfully sued the Miami City Commission to overturn the June 2025 ordinance postponing the election, winning at the 11th Judicial Circuit and the FL 3rd DCA; election proceeded Nov 4 + Dec 9 2025 (runoff). LOST runoff to Democrat Eileen Higgins 40.54% (15,099) to 59.46% (22,145). No current elected office.
